action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py finds 19905 images instead of 4879, meaning test.txt isn't found and it falls back to walking training data directory. Best score is 0.6174 from iter3. Analysis: Must fix predict.py with robust test.txt finding and debug output. Also try CLIP ViT-B/16 which may generalize better due to internet-scale contrastive pretraining. Decision: Fix predict.py, write CLIP model/training code, start CLIP training, and submit eval on existing checkpoint to verify predict.py works.
action.command: ls -la /data/ && echo '---' && head -5 /data/train.txt && echo '---' && python -c "import torch; ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('Config:', ckpt.get('config', 'N/A')); print('Val bal acc:', ckpt.get('val_bal_acc', 'N/A'))" && echo '---' && python -c "import torch; ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best_3.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('Config:', ckpt.get('config', 'N/A')); print('Val bal acc:', ckpt.get('val_bal_acc', 'N/A'))" && echo '---' && python -c "import torch; ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/swa.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('Config:', ckpt.get('config', 'N/A')); print('Val bal acc:', ckpt.get('val_bal_acc', 'N/A'))"
